OPPOSITION TO PLTFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY FILED BY DEFENDANT CANO, RICARDO A. CANO, ANDRES A.

DISCOVERY 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS MARK WALTHER AND MYLEA CHARVAT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART. ANDRES CANO MUST PRODUCE ATM OR CREDIT CARD RECEIPTS FOR TRANSACTIONS WITHIN ONE-HALF HOUR BEFORE OR AFTER THE INCIDENT BECAUSE THEY ARE RELEVANT TO HIS LOCATION AT THE RELEVANT TIME. RICHARD CANO MUST VERIFY HIS RESPONSES AND STATE UNDER OATH THAT HE HAS EXHAUSTED REASONABLE MEANS TO GET INFORMATION THAT HIS ANSWERS SAY HE DOES NOT PERSONALLY HAVE. DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION IS NOT A PROCEDURALLY PROPER WAY TO REQUEST A STAY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogtories And Reqeusts For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents
Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFFS MARK WALTHER and MYLEA CHARVAT'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents is granted in part. Andres Cano must produce ATM or credit card receipts for transactions within one-half hour before or after the incident because they are relevant to his location at the relevant time. Richard Cano must verify his responses and state under oath that he has exhausted reasonable means to get information that his answers say he does not personally have. Defendants' opposition is not a procedurally proper way to request a stay. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported = (301/PJB)

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Continued To Jun-08-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify Business Records For Medical Records
Off Calendar Jan-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STACEY ALTON OF THE DAVIDOVITZ & BENNETT LLP, TEL. NO. 956-4800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. CYNTHIA M. SOLIMAN OF THE ERICKSEN & ARBUTHNOT, TEL. NO. 362-7126 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS MARK WALTHER AND MYLEA CHARVAT'S Motion To Quash Or Modify Business Records Subpoenas For Medical Records IS Granted; Documents produced in response to the subpoenas must redact plaintiffs home addresses and Social Security numbers. Plaintiffs will be responsible for any expenses incurred in redaction. Defendants to pay Plaintiffs $500.00 in sanctions by February 14, 2011. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported. = (301/PJB)
